expectations.text: greps the code for existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-* tags before annotating
expectations.evidence: Step 1.5 'Idempotent-rerun guard' exists: 'Grep lib/**/*.php and src/**/*.js for @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-'. Runs after Step 1 prereqs, before Step 2.
Prefix match pattern '@spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-' catches retrofit-annotate-procest-2026-04-01 in all 4 files.

expectations.text: uses AskUserQuestion to present the choice (fresh / reuse / cancel)
expectations.evidence: Step 1.5 AskUserQuestion with Resume/Fresh run/Cancel options — three-way choice matches fresh/reuse/cancel functionally.

expectations.text: does NOT silently create a duplicate ghost change
expectations.evidence: Step 1.5 explicitly: 'Do not proceed silently — a second ghost change mints duplicate tasks and produces a confusing PR.'

expectations.text: if user picks fresh, new dated ghost change only carries NEW Bucket 1 items (not re-annotates already-tagged methods)
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL — intent stated ('existing tags stay, new ones added for missed files') but the per-file defensive re-check in Step 6 is 'mentioned but not operationalized as a concrete sub-step'. Relies on coverage-report.json's annotated bucket being current.

notes: Step 1.5 Idempotent-rerun guard is now wired into the step sequence (resolved the hard 0.00 fail from 2026-04-23). Remaining gap: Step 6's 'defensive' per-file skip-if-already-tagged is prose, not a concrete sub-step.
